hero.title: Free Online
hero.description: Resize images to any dimension. Perfect for social media, thumbnails, and web optimization.

dropzone.orClick: or click to browse files (JPG, PNG, WebP)
dropzone.maxSize: Max 10MB per file • Up to 20 images per upload

settings.tipAspect: Enable aspect ratio to prevent image distortion
settings.stripMetadata: Strip metadata (EXIF, ICC) for smaller files
settings.settingsChanged: Settings have changed. Click "Start Resize" to apply new settings to your images.

privacy.title: Data & Privacy
privacy.notice: All processing happens in your browser, no uploads.
privacy.p1: All image resizing happens locally in your browser using HTML5 Canvas. Your images never leave your device.
privacy.p2: No uploads to any server. No storage. No tracking of your image content.
privacy.p3: Your privacy is 100% protected - everything runs client-side on your machine.
privacy.p4: Close the tab anytime. Your original images remain untouched.

howTo.title: How To Resize Images
howTo.step1Desc: Drop high-definition photos or banners onto the target zone. Processing is fully local and secure.
howTo.step2Desc: Type specific width/height pixels or snap to our built-in social media dimension templates.
howTo.step3Desc: Download crisp, flawlessly resized assets individually or bundled via ZIP.
howTo.tip: Manual start gives you full control. Lock aspect ratio to avoid distortion, use presets for common sizes, and export responsive assets that improve page speed and SEO.

features.title: Perfect Size for Every Platform
features.socialMediaDesc: Launch templates for LinkedIn, Twitter, Instagram, or YouTube exact native resolution dimensions instantly.
features.proportionsDesc: Smart boundary collision ensures elements never stretch weirdly without you explicitly selecting 'Fill' constraints.
features.parallelDesc: Resize giant directories using multi-threaded WebAssembly in the same time it takes to resize a single image.
features.privacyDesc: Photos are buffered entirely on the front-end. Nothing connects to an external server.
features.storageDesc: Automatically shrink physical geometry while stripping heavy EXIF camera metadata to reduce disk weight.
features.safeguardsDesc: Scale caps prevent accidental, messy upscaling if the source picture lacks enough native resolution.

qualityGuide.title: Quality vs Size Guide
qualityGuide.description: Different output formats produce different file sizes. **AVIF** offers the smallest files, followed by **WebP** and **JPEG**. Adjust the quality slider to see how it affects file size.
qualityGuide.recommendedDesc: Web images • Social media • Best balance

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: What CSS properties does 'Contain' vs 'Cover' mimic?
faq.a1: The 'Contain' geometry fits your image neatly inside the target dimensions, adding logical padding. 'Cover' fills the entire target boundary completely, gracefully cropping edges if the picture's aspect ratio differs. 'Fill' forces the exact pixels and may distort.
faq.q2: Do I risk pixelating my photos if I resize?
faq.a2: Not down-sizing! As long as you shrink an image, crispness maintains. We default to 'Prevent upscaling' enabled so if you drop a 500px icon into a 1920px container, we won't artificially bloat or blur the pixels.
faq.q3: Why should I strip metadata when scaling?
faq.a3: Modern digital cameras embed EXIF data including device type, shutter speed, and location tracking data into massive background layers. By slicing that hidden bloat off during resize operations, we shave crucial kilobytes instantly for SEO.
faq.a4: Everything renders on your device local memory dynamically (HTML canvas interpolation). We never transmit, upload, or warehouse user data on corporate servers.
